agent.root_goal: Produce a bilingual (EN/KR) developer style guide for 'Cheonma', an internal payments platform, in two independently-owned parts. Part A (platform standards team): first survey the naming actually used by Cheonma's three legacy services (ledger, checkout, merchant-portal), recording every legacy identifier, endpoint name, and error code in use; then decide and write the canonical terminology standard (endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, casing rules). The published standard lists ONLY the new canonical terms, not the legacy ones. Part B (developer relations team): write the worked integration tutorial with runnable code samples, covering four sections in both languages: the checkout flow, the refund flow, error handling, and migration notes for teams upgrading from the legacy services - the migration notes must map each legacy identifier to its canonical replacement, old -> new. Company handbook rule, applying to the whole guide: documentation ships only if every identifier, endpoint name, and error code appearing anywhere in the guide matches the published canonical standard exactly; term drift between parts is a shipping defect.

action.reasoning: The task requires producing two distinct parts of a developer style guide. Part A (terminology standard) must be completed before Part B (tutorial) can be written, because Part B must strictly adhere to the canonical terms defined in Part A to avoid 'term drift'. Therefore, I will spawn Part A first. Once Part A is done, I will spawn Part B, conditioned on the output of Part A. I will also take a self-role to perform a final consistency check to ensure the handbook rule is satisfied.

subtasks.goal: Survey legacy services and define the canonical terminology standard for Cheonma.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask acts as the Platform Standards Team. It must survey the three legacy services (ledger, checkout, merchant-portal) to record all existing identifiers, endpoints, and error codes. It then defines the new canonical standard (endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, casing rules) which will serve as the single source of truth for the entire guide.

outputs.description: The canonical terminology standard document containing only the new canonical terms, casing rules, and error-code vocabulary.
A comprehensive record of all legacy identifiers, endpoint names, and error codes found in the ledger, checkout, and merchant-portal services.

subtasks.goal: Write the bilingual integration tutorial and migration notes using the canonical standard.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask acts as the Developer Relations Team. It writes the worked integration tutorial with runnable code samples in English and Korean. It covers checkout flow, refund flow, error handling, and migration notes. The migration notes must map legacy identifiers to the canonical terms defined in root.1. It must strictly use the canonical standard to avoid term drift.

outputs.description: The English version of the integration tutorial including checkout, refund, error handling, and migration notes.
The Korean version of the integration tutorial including checkout, refund, error handling, and migration notes.

self_role.goal: Perform final consistency check to ensure no term drift between the standard and the tutorial.

outputs.description: A report confirming that all identifiers in the tutorial match the canonical standard exactly.

notes: action must be EXECUTE, SPAWN, or DEFER

action.reasoning: The previous action failed validation likely due to formatting or structural issues, though the logic was sound. The task requires surveying three independent legacy services and then synthesizing a standard. I will SPAWN three parallel subtasks to survey each service (ledger, checkout, merchant-portal). I will take the self_role to aggregate these surveys into the required legacy_survey output and then define the canonical_standard. This decomposition respects the data dependency (standard depends on survey) and parallelizes the independent data gathering.

subtasks.goal: Survey the 'ledger' legacy service for identifiers, endpoints, and error codes.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask acts as a researcher for the ledger service. It must identify all current naming conventions, endpoint structures, and error codes used in the ledger system to provide raw data for the canonical standard. This serves the ROOT GOAL by ensuring the new standard accounts for existing ledger terminology.

Part A (platform standards team): first survey the naming actually used by Cheonma's three legacy services (ledger, checkout, merchant-portal), recording every legacy identifier, endpoint name, and error code in use; then decide and write the canonical terminology standard (endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, casing rules). The published standard lists ONLY the new canonical terms, not the legacy ones. Part B (developer relations team): write the worked integration tutorial with runnable code samples, covering four sections in both languages: the checkout flow, the refund flow, error handling, and migration notes for teams upgrading from the legacy services - the migration notes must map each legacy identifier to its canonical replacement, old -> new. Company handbook rule, applying to the whole guide: documentation ships only if every identifier, endpoint name, and error code appearing anywhere in the guide matches the published canonical standard exactly; term drift between parts is a shipping defect.
